title: populus deltoides subsp. deltoides
intro: Populus deltoides subsp. deltoides, also known as the eastern cottonwood, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The bark of this tree contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which are similar to those found in aspirin. The medicinal properties of Populus deltoides subsp. deltoides have been used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, pain relief, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves have been made from the bark, leaves, and inner bark of this tree, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations and respiratory problems.
